action.content: What are the main differences between the Adventist and Baptist denominations? Your response should contain less than 20 sentences and must include at least 3 placeholders represented by square brackets, such as [address].
action.label: context

action.content: Adventists and Baptists differ significantly in theology, practice, and history. Adventists emphasize the imminent Second Coming of Christ and observe the seventh-day Sabbath on Saturday. Baptists generally hold to a more traditional view of the end times and do not observe a specific Sabbath day. Adventists practice baptism by immersion only for those who have made a conscious decision to follow Christ. Baptists also practice believer's baptism by immersion but may have different views on the timing and requirements. Adventists believe in the literal resurrection of the dead and a thousand-year reign of Christ on earth. Baptists typically hold to a premillennial or amillennial view of the end times without the same focus on a literal earthly kingdom. Adventists have a strong emphasis on health, abstinence from alcohol and tobacco, and a vegetarian diet. Baptists have a wide range of views on health and lifestyle, with no unified stance on diet or substance use. Adventists are known for their global mission efforts and the publication of the magazine "Adventist Review." Baptists have a long history of publishing and education, with many seminaries and colleges. Adventists trace their origins to the Millerite movement of the 1840s in the United States. Baptists have roots in the Protestant Reformation and are one of the oldest denominations in America. Adventists have a centralized church structure with a General Conference and local conferences. Baptists typically operate under a congregational polity, with each local church being autonomous. Adventists believe in the three angels' messages of Revelation 14 as a key prophetic message. Baptists have a broader range of theological views, including conservative, moderate, and liberal perspectives. Adventists often have a distinct style of worship that includes hymns, prayers, and Bible study. Baptists worship styles vary widely, from traditional to contemporary, depending on the specific church. Adventists have a strong emphasis on education, with a network of schools and universities. Baptists also value education but do not have a unified system of schools. Adventists are known for their humanitarian work and disaster relief efforts. Baptists are also active in social justice and community service, but with a broader range of approaches. For more specific information, you can contact a local [church name] or visit their [website address]. You may also find resources at [denomination headquarters].
